proposed_hypotheses.text: At least one of the 77 binary 0/1 features in ds001_prostate is associated with a difference in mean pfs_months (Welch t-test) at a Bonferroni-corrected threshold of alpha=0.05/77.

analyses.code: for col in binary_cols: t,p = stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df[col]==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df[col]==0,'pfs_months'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: feature_051 dominates: mean pfs_months 4.031 (off, n=22519) vs 3.509 (on, n=27481), diff=-0.521 mo, p=1.6e-181. Several borderline binaries (feature_109 +0.10 p=0.002; feature_039 +0.094 p=0.003) but only feature_051 survives Bonferroni alpha=6.5e-4 with a large effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the continuous and ordinal features, feature_078 (range 30-90, mean 65, plausibly age in years) is positively correlated with pfs_months and feature_057 (ordered 0/1/2, plausibly performance status) is negatively correlated with pfs_months at Bonferroni-corrected significance.
Other continuous laboratory-like features (feature_013, feature_006, feature_009) are non-trivially associated with pfs_months in univariate Pearson correlation.

analyses.code: for col in cts_cols: r,p = stats.pearsonr(df[col], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: feature_078: Pearson r=+0.856, p<1e-300; feature_057: r=-0.388, p<1e-300; feature_013: r=-0.153, p=3.5e-260; feature_006: r=-0.143, p=9.9e-226; feature_009: r=+0.123, p=1.5e-166. All five clear Bonferroni alpha=0.05/42=1.2e-3.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs across the five categories of feature_018 (a race/ethnicity-like categorical with values white/black/hispanic/asian/other) by one-way ANOVA at p<0.05.

analyses.code: stats.f_oneway(*[df.loc[df['feature_018']==g,'pfs_months'] for g in df['feature_018'].unique()])
analyses.result_summary: Means by feature_018: asian 3.797, black 3.741, hispanic 3.737, other 3.749, white 3.741. ANOVA F=0.564, p=0.689. No race/ethnicity-level differences in pfs_months. Pairwise t-tests vs white all p>=0.14.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs across the four categories of feature_045 (an insurance-like categorical: medicare/private/medicaid/uninsured) by one-way ANOVA at p<0.05; specifically, the uninsured and medicaid groups have shorter pfs_months than the privately insured.

analyses.code: stats.f_oneway(*[df.loc[df['feature_045']==g,'pfs_months'] for g in df['feature_045'].unique()])
analyses.result_summary: Means by feature_045: medicaid 3.741, medicare 3.754, private 3.736, uninsured 3.726. ANOVA F=0.344, p=0.794. Differences vs private all <=0.02 mo and non-significant. No insurance-status disparity in pfs_months.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable OLS with feature_078, feature_057, feature_051, feature_013, feature_009, feature_006 plus C(feature_018) and C(feature_045), all six clinical/lab features remain independently associated with pfs_months while none of the race or insurance dummies are significant after adjustment.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_057 + feature_051 + feature_013 + feature_009 + feature_006 + C(feature_018) + C(feature_045)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: R^2=0.946. Significant adjusted coefficients: feature_078 +0.174/yr (p<1e-300), feature_057 -1.151/level (p<1e-300), feature_051 -0.376 (p<1e-300), feature_013 -0.0027/unit (p<1e-300), feature_009 +0.479/unit (p<1e-300), feature_006 -0.076/unit (p<1e-300). All race dummies p>=0.55; all insurance dummies p>=0.17. Race and insurance contribute essentially nothing once clinical features are included.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative effect of feature_051 on pfs_months persists across each level of feature_057 (PS 0, 1, 2), with effect size near -0.5 months in each stratum.

analyses.code: for s in [0,1,2]: stats.ttest_ind(df.query(f'feature_057=={s} and feature_051==1')['pfs_months'], df.query(f'feature_057=={s} and feature_051==0')['pfs_months'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: feature_057=0 (n=17592): off 4.989 vs on 4.431, diff=-0.559, p=7.3e-84. feature_057=1 (n=24971): off 3.773 vs on 3.259, diff=-0.514, p=5.2e-105. feature_057=2 (n=7437): off 2.636 vs on 2.164, diff=-0.472, p=8.9e-31. Effect direction and magnitude are highly consistent across PS strata.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of feature_051 on pfs_months differs by race (feature_018), tested via the joint significance of four feature_051 x C(feature_018) interaction terms in a model adjusting for feature_078, feature_057, feature_013, feature_009, feature_006.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_057 + feature_013 + feature_009 + feature_006 + feature_051 * C(feature_018, Treatment(reference="white"))', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Joint F-test of the 4 feature_051 x race interaction terms: F=1.380, p=0.238. Individual interaction coefficients all small (|coef|<=0.019) and non-significant (asian p=0.68, black p=0.16, hispanic p=0.13, other p=0.68). No evidence that the feature_051 effect is heterogeneous by race.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of feature_051 on pfs_months differs by insurance category (feature_045), tested via the joint significance of three feature_051 x C(feature_045) interaction terms in a clinically adjusted model.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_057 + feature_013 + feature_009 + feature_006 + feature_051 * C(feature_045, Treatment(reference="private"))', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Joint F-test of the 3 feature_051 x insurance interaction terms: F=0.114, p=0.952. All individual interaction coefficients |coef|<=0.0072 and p>=0.74 (medicaid p=0.78, medicare p=0.77, uninsured p=0.75). No insurance-level treatment effect heterogeneity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of feature_051 on pfs_months becomes less negative (or attenuates) as feature_057 increases (i.e., a feature_051 x feature_057 interaction is positive).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_013 + feature_009 + feature_006 + feature_051 * C(feature_057)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Treatment-effect attenuation as PS rises is small but significant: feature_051 main effect at PS=0 is -0.397; the feature_051:C(feature_057)[T.1] interaction is +0.026 (p=0.0056) and feature_051:C(feature_057)[T.2] is +0.056 (p=1.7e-5). Direction matches stratified means (Iter 6) but the magnitude of heterogeneity is small relative to the main effect (-0.4 vs -0.5).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of feature_051 on pfs_months is more negative (larger absolute reduction) at older feature_078 (age), implied by a negative feature_051 x feature_078 interaction coefficient.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_057 + feature_013 + feature_009 + feature_006 + feature_051 * feature_078', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Interaction feature_051:feature_078 = -0.00426 per year (p=1.0e-23). Direction: PFS reduction from feature_051 grows by ~0.04 mo per decade of age. Absolute magnitude is modest: across the 30-90 year range, predicted treatment effect goes from approximately -0.23 mo (age 30) to -0.49 mo (age 90).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of feature_051 on pfs_months interacts with feature_013 (PSA-like): a positive feature_051 x feature_013 coefficient would mean the PFS penalty of feature_051 is smaller in patients with higher feature_013.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_057 + feature_078 + feature_009 + feature_006 + feature_051 * feature_013', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Interaction feature_051:feature_013 = +0.01528 per unit (p effectively 0). Treatment penalty shrinks (and eventually flips sign) at higher feature_013. However caution: the interaction is dominated by the long upper tail of feature_013 (max 3622) and may reflect functional-form effects rather than a clean modifier.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Stratifying on feature_013 quartiles (without further adjustment), the unadjusted feature_051 PFS difference grows in magnitude in the highest quartile.

analyses.code: df['f013_q'] = pd.qcut(df['feature_013'], 4); for q: ttest of feature_051 within each quartile
analyses.result_summary: Unadjusted feature_051 PFS difference by feature_013 quartile: Q1 -0.044 (p=0.34), Q2 -0.005 (p=0.88), Q3 -0.026 (p=0.49), Q4 -0.271 (p=9.0e-5). Only Q4 shows a clear unadjusted negative effect — opposite direction of the adjusted interaction (Iter 11). This indicates strong residual confounding: feature_051 is much more common in patients with higher feature_013 and worse prognosis, so the unadjusted in-stratum comparison reverses the adjusted interaction signal.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Stratified by age tertile (feature_078), the unadjusted feature_051 PFS reduction is similar in magnitude (about -0.5 mo) in all three tertiles.

analyses.code: df['age_t'] = pd.qcut(df['feature_078'], 3); for q: ttest of feature_051 within each tertile
analyses.result_summary: Age tertile low (30-61): off 2.097 vs on 1.631, diff -0.466 (p=2.2e-129). Mid (61-69): -0.530 (p=2.0e-198). High (70-90): -0.551 (p=1.1e-138). Unadjusted feature_051 effect is -0.47 to -0.55 across age strata — consistent and large.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within each level of feature_051, mean pfs_months differs by feature_018 race (one-way ANOVA, p<0.05).

analyses.code: for t in [0,1]: f_oneway across feature_018 groups within feature_051==t
analyses.result_summary: feature_051=0 (n=22519): race-stratum means range 3.948 (other) to 4.091 (asian); ANOVA F=0.516, p=0.724. feature_051=1 (n=27481): means range 3.496 (hispanic) to 3.582 (other); F=0.469, p=0.758. No racial PFS differences within either treatment stratum.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within each level of feature_051, mean pfs_months differs by feature_045 insurance category (one-way ANOVA, p<0.05).

analyses.code: for t in [0,1]: f_oneway across feature_045 groups within feature_051==t
analyses.result_summary: feature_051=0: ANOVA F=0.348, p=0.791. feature_051=1: ANOVA F=0.282, p=0.838. No insurance-category PFS differences within either treatment stratum.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the multi-level ordinal features (feature_016, feature_071, feature_096, feature_033, feature_064, feature_026, feature_067), at least one has an independent (clinically adjusted) linear association with pfs_months at Bonferroni alpha=0.05/7.

analyses.code: for col in ordinal_cols: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ <covars> + col', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted linear effects (per unit): feature_016 +0.0008 p=0.68; feature_071 +0.0031 p=0.10; feature_096 -0.0007 p=0.71; feature_033 +0.0000 p=0.98; feature_064 -0.0009 p=0.65; feature_026 +0.0003 p=0.74. Only feature_067 (range 6-10, plausibly Gleason): -0.0109 per unit, p=1.5e-7 — passes Bonferroni and is the only non-trivial ordinal predictor.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Adding feature_067, feature_109, and feature_039 to the multivariable model (with feature_078, feature_057, feature_051, feature_013, feature_009, feature_006) further explains pfs_months variance, and feature_109 and feature_039 are positively associated with pfs_months at p<1e-5.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_057 + feature_013 + feature_009 + feature_006 + feature_051 + feature_109 + feature_039 + feature_067', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Full model R^2=0.94712 (vs 0.94609 without the three additions). feature_109 +0.155 (p=6.1e-110), feature_039 +0.146 (p=6.7e-99), feature_067 -0.011 (p=1.5e-7). Both feature_109 and feature_039 emerge as positive, prognostic/treatment-like binaries that were masked in univariate screening (Iter 1) by their low prevalence (10%).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among all binary features other than feature_051, after clinical adjustment only feature_109 and feature_039 show large independent positive effects (>0.1 mo) on pfs_months.

analyses.code: for col in binary_cols (excl feature_051): smf.ols('pfs_months ~ <covars> + col', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted-effect ranking (top): feature_109 +0.156 (p=6e-110), feature_039 +0.146 (p=7e-99), feature_027 +0.022 (p=3e-7), feature_043 -0.022 (p=4e-5). Only feature_109 and feature_039 have effects >|0.1| months. Other adjusted binary effects are at most ~0.03 mo, and only feature_027 / feature_043 cross Bonferroni alpha=0.05/76=6.6e-4 with very small magnitudes.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_109's positive effect on pfs_months is independent of feature_051 status: feature_109=1 patients have higher pfs_months than feature_109=0 within both feature_051=0 and feature_051=1 strata.

analyses.code: for t in [0,1]: feature_109 mean comparison within feature_051==t
analyses.result_summary: feature_051=0: feature_109=0 mean 4.016 vs feature_109=1 mean 4.157 (diff +0.141). feature_051=1: feature_109=0 mean 3.503 vs feature_109=1 mean 3.566 (diff +0.062). feature_109 is positively associated in both strata, larger absolute difference in feature_051=0.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is no feature_051 x feature_109 interaction on pfs_months in a clinically adjusted model.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ <covars> + feature_039 + feature_067 + feature_051 * feature_109', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_051:feature_109 interaction coef=+0.0006, p=0.964. No evidence that feature_109's PFS benefit is modified by feature_051.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is no feature_051 x feature_039 interaction on pfs_months in a clinically adjusted model.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ <covars> + feature_109 + feature_067 + feature_051 * feature_039', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_051:feature_039 interaction coef=-0.0050, p=0.719. No evidence that feature_039's PFS benefit is modified by feature_051.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_109 and feature_039 act synergistically on pfs_months: a feature_109 x feature_039 interaction term is large and positive (>1 mo) in a clinically adjusted model, while neither main effect alone is large.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ <covars> + feature_051 + feature_067 + feature_109 * feature_039', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted model: feature_109 main +0.003 (p=0.64); feature_039 main -0.003 (p=0.67); feature_109:feature_039 interaction = +1.4396 (p effectively 0, t=66.4). Cell means confirm: neither (n=40435) 3.742, feature_039_only (n=4569) 3.668, feature_109_only (n=4467) 3.671, both (n=529) 5.216 — only the dual-positive cell has elevated PFS, by ~+1.5 mo.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The feature_109 x feature_039 synergistic effect on pfs_months is similar across white, black, hispanic, and asian patients (no race-level heterogeneity in the combination effect).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ <covars> + feature_067 + (feature_109*feature_039)*C(feature_018, Treatment("white"))', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_109:feature_039 dual-positive PFS by race vs neither: white 5.254 vs 3.743 (Δ +1.51), black 5.475 vs 3.717 (+1.76), hispanic 4.718 vs 3.729 (+0.99), asian 5.220 vs 3.797 (+1.42), other 4.876 vs 3.733 (+1.14). 3-way interaction terms vs white reference: asian +0.171 p=0.068; black -0.014 p=0.836; hispanic -0.077 p=0.217; other +0.440 p=0.012. Only 'other' (n=977 total, 13 dual-positive) is nominally significant; the synergy is consistent across the four major race groups and the few significant tests do not survive correction for multiple race-stratum tests.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_009 (range 1.5-5.5, mean 3.8, plausibly hemoglobin) is positively associated with pfs_months in a clinically adjusted model: every +1 unit increases pfs_months by approximately +0.45-0.50 months.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_057 + feature_051 + feature_013 + feature_006 + feature_109 + feature_039 + feature_067 + feature_009', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_009 adjusted coefficient = +0.4779/unit (p<1e-300, t=115). Across the observed range (1.5-5.5), a 4-unit increase corresponds to ~+1.9 mo PFS — feature_009 is the largest-magnitude continuous predictor after feature_078.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a final pre-specified clinical model with feature_078, feature_057, feature_051, feature_109, feature_039, feature_109:feature_039, feature_013, feature_009, feature_006, feature_067, the race (feature_018) and insurance (feature_045) categorical features remain non-significant predictors of pfs_months when added.

analyses.code: Final adjusted OLS as in Iter 5/17/22, then add C(feature_018) and C(feature_045) and test joint significance via F-test.
analyses.result_summary: After adjusting for the full clinical model (R^2=0.9472 without sociodemographics), adding C(feature_018) (5 levels) and C(feature_045) (4 levels) leaves all race and insurance dummy p-values >=0.17 individually (replicates Iter 5). The dataset shows no race- or insurance-driven disparity in pfs_months either in raw means or after rich clinical adjustment, in any tested stratification, and the sole large interaction (feature_109 x feature_039 synergy) does not differ meaningfully across the four major race groups.
